message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "IX and X",
    "grade": "9, 10",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2025-26",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "raw_nodes": [
    {
      "title": "SCIENCE Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26)",
      "level": "root",
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "1 SCIENCE Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26) Science Education aims to achieve Scientific understanding of the natural and physical world; Capacities for scientific inquiry; Understanding the evolution of scientific knowledge; Interdisciplinary understanding between science and other curricular areas; Understanding of the relationship between Science, Technology and, Society; Scientific temper and Creativity. The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes viz. Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources."
    },
    {
      "title": "Curricular Goals",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The Curricular Goals of Science at the Secondary Stage move from the concrete nature of the Middle Stage towards abstraction - from perceptual and practical concepts to theoretical concepts. The Learning Standards (Curricular Goals and Competencies) for Science as an integrated curricular area, in alignment with the National Curriculum Framework 2023 are as follows: CG - 1 Explores the world of matter... C - 7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum..."
    },
    {
      "title": "General Instructions for Assessment",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "It is important to note that the Curricular Goals are interdependent... General Instructions for Assessment: 1. There will be an Annual Examination based on the entire syllabus. 2. The Annual Examination will be of 80 marks and 20 marks weightage shall be for Internal Assessment."
    },
    {
      "title": "Internal Assessment Details",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "For Internal Assessment: i) There will be Periodic Assessment that would include... ii) For 5 marks - Practical / Laboratory work... iii) For 5 marks - Portfolio..."
    },
    {
      "title": "COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS IX (2025-26)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour 25 II Organization in the Living World 22 III Motion, Force and Work 27 IV Food; Food Production 06 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Materials Unit I",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Matter in Our Surroundings... Is Matter Around Us Pure... Atoms and Molecules..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Formative Content (Class IX)",
      "level": 3,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments... Health and Diseases..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Moving Things Unit III",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Motion... Force and Newton's laws... Gravitation... Floatation... Work, Energy and Power... Sound:"
    },
    {
      "title": "Formative Content (Class IX Unit III)",
      "level": 3,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments... Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Food Unit IV",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Note for Teachers (Class IX)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination."
    },
    {
      "title": "PRACTICALS Class IX",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes. (LIST OF EXPERIMENTS) 1. Preparation of... 2. Preparation of..."
    },
    {
      "title": "COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS X (2025-26)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour 25 II World of Living 25 III Natural Phenomena 12 IV Effects of Current 13 V Natural Resources 05 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Materials Unit I (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Chemical Reactions and Equations... Acids, Bases and Salts... Metals and Non-metals... Carbon and its Compounds..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: The World of the Living Unit II (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Life processes... Control and co-ordination in animals and plants... Reproduction... Heredity and Evolution..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Natural Phenomena Unit III (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Reflection of light by curved surfaces... Refraction... Power of a lens. Functioning of a lens in human eye..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: How Things Work Unit IV (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Electric current... Ohm's law... Magnetic effects of current... Direct current. Alternating current..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Natural Resources Unit V (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Note for Teachers (Class X)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination."
    },
    {
      "title": "PRACTICALS Class X",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Practical should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes. LIST OF EXPERIMENTS 1. A. Finding the pH..."
    },
    {
      "title": "PRESCRIBED BOOKS",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Science-Textbook for class IX-NCERT Publication... Laboratory Manual-Science-Class X, NCERT Publication"
    },
    {
      "title": "Question Paper Design (Theory) Class X",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Competencies Total Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding 50 % Application of Knowledge/Concepts 30 % Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create 20 %"
    },
    {
      "title": "Internal Assessment (Class X)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Periodic Assessment - 05 marks + 05 marks Subject Enrichment (Practical Work) - 05 marks Portfolio - 05 marks"
    }
  ],
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "IX",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination Structure",
      "raw_nodes": [],
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Matter in Our Surroundings", "Is Matter Around Us Pure", "Atoms and Molecules"],
          "headings": [
            "Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter: solid, liquid and gas and their characteristics; change of state- melting (absorption of heat), freezing, evaporation (cooling by evaporation), condensation, sublimation.",
            "Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions. Physical and chemical changes (excluding separating the components of a mixture); Pure and Impure substances.",
            "Atoms and molecules, Law of Chemical Combination, Chemical formula of common compounds, Atomic and molecular masses. Structure of atom: Sub-atomic particles: Electrons, protons and neutrons, Models of atom; Valency, Atomic Number and Mass Number, Isotopes and Isobars."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Matter in Our Surroundings",
            "Is Matter Around Us Pure",
            "Atoms and Molecules"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "States of matter, change of state",
            "Mixtures (heterogeneous/homogenous), colloids, suspensions",
            "Physical and chemical changes",
            "Sub-atomic particles, Models of atom, Isotopes and Isobars"
          ],
          "key_concepts": [
            "Particulate Nature of Matter",
            "Law of Chemical Combination",
            "Atomic Structure (Bohr's model)",
            "Valency"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Organization in the Living World",
          "marks": 22,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Cell - Basic Unit of life", "Tissues, Organs, Organ System, Organism"],
          "headings": [
            "Cell as a basic unit of life; prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, multicellular organisms; cell membrane and cell wall, cell organelles and cell inclusions; chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus; nucleus, chromosomes - basic structure, number.",
            "Structure and functions of animal and plant tissues (only four types of tissues in animals; Meristematic and Permanent tissues in plants)."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Cell Structure",
            "Tissues"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic cells",
            "Organelles (Nucleus, Mitochondria, etc.)",
            "Animal tissues (4 types)",
            "Plant tissues (Meristematic and Permanent)"
          ],
          "key_concepts": [
            "Cell as basic unit of life",
            "Semi-permeability of cell membrane"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Motion, Force and Work",
          "marks": 27,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Motion", "Force and Newton's laws", "Gravitation", "Floatation", "Work, Energy and Power", "Sound"],
          "headings": [
            "Distance and displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion along a straight line; acceleration, distance-time and velocity-time graphs for uniform motion and uniformly accelerated motion, elementary idea of uniform circular motion.",
            "Force and Motion, Newton's Laws of Motion, Action and Reaction forces, Inertia of a body, Inertia and mass, Momentum, Force and Acceleration.",
            "Gravitation; Universal Law of Gravitation, Force of Gravitation of the earth (gravity), Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall.",
            "Thrust and Pressure. Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy.",
            "Work done by a Force, Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Lawof conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit of Energy).",
            "Nature of sound and its propagation in various media, speed of sound, range of hearing in humans; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Motion",
            "Force and Newton's Laws",
            "Gravitation",
            "Floatation",
            "Work Energy Power",
            "Sound"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Distance, displacement, velocity-time graphs",
            "Newton's laws (1st, 2nd, 3rd)",
            "Universal Law of Gravitation",
            "Archimedes' Principle",
            "Conservation of Energy",
            "Reflection and Echo"
          ],
          "key_concepts": [
            "Uniform Circular Motion",
            "Inertia",
            "Acceleration due to Gravity",
            "Buoyancy",
            "Law of Conservation of Energy"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Food; Food Production",
          "marks": 6,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Plant and animal breeding"],
          "headings": [
            "Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management; Use of fertilizersand manures; Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Food Production"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Breeding",
            "Fertilizers and Manures",
            "Pest protection",
            "Organic farming"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "X",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination Structure",
      "raw_nodes": [],
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Chemical Reactions and Equations", "Acids, Bases and Salts", "Metals and Non-metals", "Carbon and its Compounds"],
          "headings": [
            "Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction.",
            "Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH– ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale... importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris.",
            "Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention.",
            "Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature... Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid..."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Chemical Reactions",
            "Acids Bases Salts",
            "Metals Non-metals",
            "Carbon Compounds"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Types of reactions (Combination, Decomposition, etc.)",
            "pH scale and neutralization",
            "Reactivity series",
            "Covalent bonds, Hydrocarbons, Functional groups"
          ],
          "key_concepts": [
            "Balanced Chemical Equations",
            "Oxidation-Reduction",
            "Ionic Compounds",
            "Homologous Series"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "World of Living",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Life processes", "Control and co-ordination in animals and plants", "Reproduction", "Heredity and Evolution"],
          "headings": [
            "'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals.",
            "Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones.",
            "Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health.",
            "Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Life Processes",
            "Control and Co-ordination",
            "Reproduction",
            "Heredity and Evolution"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Nutrition, Respiration, Transport, Excretion",
            "Nervous system, Plant hormones",
            "Family planning, Reproductive health",
            "Mendel's Laws, Sex determination"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Natural Phenomena",
          "marks": 12,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Reflection of light by curved surfaces", "Refraction"],
          "headings": [
            "Images formed by spherical mirrors, centre of curvature, principal axis, principal focus, focal length, mirror formula (Derivation not required), magnification.",
            "Laws of refraction, refractive index. Refraction of light by spherical lens; Image formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens. Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections...",
            "Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light..."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Reflection",
            "Refraction",
            "Lenses"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Spherical mirrors (Concave/Convex)",
            "Mirror formula, Magnification",
            "Lens formula, Power of lens",
            "Dispersion and Scattering"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Effects of Current",
          "marks": 13,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Electric current", "Ohm's law", "Magnetic effects of current"],
          "headings": [
            "Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.",
            "Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors... Heating effect of electric current... Electric power...",
            "Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule..."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Electric Current",
            "Ohm's Law and Resistance",
            "Magnetic Effects"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Series/Parallel combinations",
            "Heating effect, Electric power (P=VI)",
            "Fleming's Left Hand Rule",
            "AC vs DC"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Natural Resources",
          "marks": 5,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Our environment"],
          "headings": [
            "Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions. Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances."
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Environment"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Ozone depletion",
            "Waste management",
            "Biodegradable vs Non-biodegradable"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [
    {
      "title": "Health and Diseases (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Health and its failure. Infectious and Non-infectious diseases, their causes and manifestation. Diseases caused by microbes (Virus, Bacteria and Protozoans) and their prevention; Principles of treatment and prevention. Pulse Polio programmes.",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World",
      "related_chapter": "Cell - Basic Unit of life / Tissues",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },
    {
      "title": "Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work",
      "related_chapter": "Force and Newton's laws",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    }
  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Preparation of solutions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "1. Preparation of: Unit-I a) a true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) a suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand in water c) a colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk in water",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Is Matter Around Us Pure"
    },
    {
      "title": "Preparation of mixture/compound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "2. Preparation of Unit-I a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Is Matter Around Us Pure"
    },
    {
      "title": "Physical/Chemical changes reactions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "3. Perform the following reactions... Iron with copper sulphate solution... Burning of magnesium ribbon...",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Atoms and Molecules"
    },
    {
      "title": "Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of (a) onion peel, (b) human cheek cells",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Cell - Basic Unit of life"
    },
    {
      "title": "Identification of tissues (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenyma tissues in plants... muscle fibers...",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Tissues"
    },
    {
      "title": "Melting/Boiling point determination (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Atoms and Molecules"
    },
    {
      "title": "Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Sound"
    },
    {
      "title": "Density determination (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "8. Determination of the density of solid... by using a spring balance...",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Gravitation"
    },
    {
      "title": "Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed...",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Floatation"
    },
    {
      "title": "Speed of pulse propagation (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Sound"
    },
    {
      "title": "Conservation of mass verification (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Atoms and Molecules"
    },
    {
      "title": "pH determination (Class X)",
      "source_text": "1. A. Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Acids, Bases and Salts"
    },
    {
      "title": "Properties of acids/bases reactions (Class X)",
      "source_text": "2. Performing and observing the following reactions... Action of water on quicklime...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Metals and Non-metals"
    },
    {
      "title": "Reactivity series experiment (Class X)",
      "source_text": "3. Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Metals and Non-metals"
    },
    {

      "related_unit": "Effects of Current (Unit IV)",
      "related_chapter": "Ohm's law"

      "related_unit": "Effects of Current (Unit IV)",
      "related_chapter": "Series combination"
    },
    {

      "source_text": "6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Life processes"
    },
    {
      "title": "CO2 in respiration experiment (Class X)",
      "source_text": "7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Life processes"
    },
    {
      "title": "Acetic acid properties (Class X)",
      "source_text": "8. Study of the following properties of acetic acid... Odour, solubility in water...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Carbon and its Compounds"
    },
    {
      "title": "Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)",
      "source_text": "9. Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": "Carbon and its Compounds"
    },
    {
      "title": "Focal length determination (Class X)",
      "source_text": "10. Determination of the focal length of: Concave mirror, Convex lens...",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Reflection/Refraction"
    },
    {
      "title": "Glass slab refraction tracing (Class X)",
      "source_text": "11. Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab...",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Refraction"
    },
    {
      "title": "Binary fission/Budding study (Class X)",
      "source_text": "12. Studying (a) binary fission in Amoeba, and (b) budding in yeast...",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Reproduction"
    },
    {
      "title": "Prism refraction tracing (Class X)",
      "source_text": "13. Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": "Refraction"
    },
    {
      "title": "Embryo identification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "14. Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed...",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": "Reproduction"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Periodic Assessment Methods",
      "source_text": "Diverse methods of assessment as per the need of the class dynamics and curriculum transaction. These may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Portfolio",
      "source_text": "For 5 marks - Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Projects in Assessment Methods",
      "source_text": "Diverse methods... projects, posters, presentations...",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Annual Examination: 80 Marks (Class IX and X)",
      "Internal Assessment: 20 Marks",
      "Grand Total: 100 Marks",
      "Periodic Assessment: 5 marks + 5 marks = 10 marks",
      "Practical/Laboratory Work: 5 marks",
      "Portfolio: 5 marks"
